title: Settling Outstanding Tax Matters and Bills for a Deceased Person
text:
Settling Outstanding Tax Matters and Bills for a Deceased Person - SupportGoWhere
Settling Outstanding Tax Matters and Bills for a Deceased Person
Share link
Enables executors or administrators to settle the deceased's taxes and outstanding debts using the estate's funds before distribution to beneficiaries.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Settle the deceased's taxes and debts using the estate's funds
Collapse all sections
About this support

Executors or administrators of a deceased can act on the deceased’s following matters:

Filing personal income tax for the deceased on the income earned up to the date of death.

Filing estate or trust income tax for the deceased on the income. Estate income refers to the income generated by the deceased’s assets after date of death, during the administration of the estate. Trust income refers to the income from assets held in trust.

Settling the taxes and other bills such as loans, funeral expenses and medical bills from the estate funds of the deceased before distribution to deceased’s beneficiaries.

Who is this for?

Executors or administrators of a deceased’s estate who are responsible for settling outstanding taxes, debts and liabilities. 

Remember, the executor or administrator must ensure all outstanding taxes, loans, funeral expenses, and medical bills are paid from the deceased's estate before distributing the remaining assets to the beneficiaries.

How to apply?

File personal income tax for the deceased

	

Provide IRAS with the following details via myTaxMail:

Name, address and identification number of the legal personal representative who is handling the affairs of the deceased;

Copy of the death certificate;

Copy of the Grant of Probate/Letter of Administration, if available;

Income details from all sources, up to the date of death (please specify the breakdown for each income source); and

Type and amount of tax relief available/applicable to reduce the tax burden.




File an Income Tax Return for Estate or Trust Income

	

Download and complete the Request Form for Form T from IRAS's website.

Email the completed form to trust@iras.gov.sg and IRAS will provide you with:

a Trust Reference Number

a copy of Form T to complete

Mail the completed Form T to this address: The Comptroller of Income Tax, 55 Newton Road, Singapore 307987.

Where can I find help?

View more details 

For guidance, consult a lawyer or contact IRAS directly at 1800 356 8300 or visit the IRAS office at 55 Newton Road, Revenue House, Singapore 307987, from Monday to Friday, 8 am to 5 pm.  

If you need to speak with a tax officer, you are encouraged to make an appointment at least 2 working days in advance.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

55 Newton Road, Revenue House, Singapore 307987

Scheme last updated 08 Dec 2025
